title: dodecatheon pulchellum subsp. macrocarpum
intro: Dodecatheon pulchellum subsp. macrocarpum, also known as the tall shooting star, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the gentian family and is native to western North America. The tall shooting star has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. It has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, or as a natural remedy for general health and well-being.
